title: Dimitar and the Blueberry Dragon Pop
summary: Dimitar, a tiny purple dragon, hatches from a small egg in a mountain nest made of gold, glitter, and dragon branches. After a rough start trying to find the perfect snack—rejecting leaves and sticks in favor of cold, refreshing blueberry raisin dragon pops—Dimitar realizes he needs a new place to live. Guided by Isabella and Taylee Mouse, the group uses a secret map to fly from the cold mountains to a lush jungle, where the little dragon prepares to build his new home.

highlightQuote.text: Maybe you'll grow up to be a mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: So Papa Mole found the ancient book about Dimitar in his attic.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: He pulled it off the shelf and it was so old, it was all dusty. He had to go [blows] to blow the dust off the cover.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And there on the cover was a picture of a tiny purple dragon named Dimitar.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so he brought the book down to Disco and Sisco, who were getting ready for bed, and he said, "Well, I think I've got one for you."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And Disco and Sisco were excited, and they snuggled up close, and Papa Mole opened the book and began to read.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Once upon a time, Papa Mole read, long ago, there was, high in the mountains, a great dragon nest. And what are dragon nests made of?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Strawberries and feathers, with gold.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: With gold?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: With gold, sparkly glitter dragon branches.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Dragon branches? Oh.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: And sea grass and dragon pests. Dragon pests are a kind of rock.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Dragon pests are a kind of rock, I see. Yeah.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so in this great big nest, there were many broken eggshells from the dragons who had already hatched.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: But there was one that was smaller than all the rest. Much, much smaller. And it had not yet hatched.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And on that morning, it was a cold morning high in the mountains. The sun rose just peaking above the horizon and lit up the clouds in orange and yellow.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And that little tiny egg did a little wobble wobble wobble wobble. And then there was a crack!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And out of the egg popped the little tiny purple face of a very smart and very mad-looking little dragon.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What are you mad about, dragon?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: [Dragon voice] He said, and in baby dragon language that means: "I need some snacks right away!"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: He's going to be huge.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And the baby dragon said, "I don't know, I was just hatched! You gotta help me!"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: How about leaves, flowers, how about dragon flowers, dragon leaves, and dragon sticks, and dragon mud?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: I'll try it! Give me a leaf!

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Here, here's a dragon flower.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Crunch crunch crunch. And a stick and some dragon mud. Crunch crunch crunch, munch munch munch. Ptooey! And he spat out the leaf. "Yucko!"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And crunch crunch munch, and he spat out the stick. "Blah!"

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then he nibbled on the flower. Crunch munch munch. "Hmm, smells nice but blah! It tastes horrible! You tricked me! These aren't dragon snacks! What's that mud doing there?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Try that mud.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: [Slurping] "Mmm, it tastes good, but wait a minute... blah! That's disgusting! That's not dragon snacks! Who are you, anyway?"

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I am Isabella the kiddo and Taylee Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You're Isabella the kiddo and who is that? Taylee? Taylee Mouse? Well, nice to meet you. I'm a dragon, and I was just hatched from my egg just a minute ago, and I'm still very hungry.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: How about dragon water, dragon, dragon, uh, dragon blueberry rays, and dragon feathers? Here you go.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Okay, I'll try those. The dragon water. [Slurping] Ah, now we're getting somewhere. That was delicious! And what was the blueberry something?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: It was a blueberry raisin dragon pop.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: A blueberry raisin dragon pop! Hey, it's the same color as me! I'm purple and it's purple. [Slurping] Oh, that's so good! It's so cold and refreshing and sweet! I love it!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And what was the other thing you had?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: A dragon, dragon, dragon, dragon piece of, a dragon, a dragon branch.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh. Crunch crunch. No, that's no good. Hey, I like mice. Could I eat that mouse?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: No, not that mouse. Here's a pretend mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Pretend mouse? Munch munch munch. Delicious, thank you!

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, if I can't eat that mouse, maybe I better talk to it. Hello, Taylee Mouse.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Hello, I like cheese.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, you do? I see. I don't have any cheese, sorry. I'm just a baby dragon.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Maybe you'll grow up to be a mouse.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: No, I'm gonna grow up to be a bigger dragon. I don't think I'm gonna be really big, but I'm gonna be medium-sized, probably.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, I feel a little better now because I had some food. But now I have my next problem.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: What is it?

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Well, I can't stay here in the nest. I have to go fly and find a new place to have a nest. I'd like to get down out of these mountains and maybe find a forest or a lake or a desert or something.

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: Here, I found, I found a jungle right there.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Right there? What do you mean?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: There's my jungle map inside of my closet. It's a jungle map picture.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh, show me the picture. Oh yeah, hey, there is a jungle right down there! Well, would you like to fly there with me? Can you fly?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I can't. My, I do have an owl costume that can.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: An owl costume? Yeah, you better put on your owl costume. What about you, Taylee?

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: I'm having the owl costumes, but I can't wear it right now because I'm getting ready for bed, and I can't sleep in that.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: You can't, that's true. And the dragon said, "Well, I think I can fly. I've never tried it yet. But Taylee, maybe you could ride on my back while I'm flying. And Izzy, you could fly with your owl costume, and let's fly down to the jungle."

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And so they lifted off into the air. Flap flap flap flap flap. And they soared high in the sky, Izzy flapping her owl wings, and the dragon flapping his purple wings with Taylee the mouse riding on his back.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And they soared in a great big circle all the way around the mountain. And the big dragon nest where the dragon had been born looked small, tiny down below.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And then they came all the way back around and, consulting Izzy's jungle map carefully, they found the location of the jungle and dove down towards it. Flap flap flap flap flap. Flying fast through the air.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: And as they went down and down and down through the sky...

transcript.speaker: Izzy
transcript.text: After that you can put back my mama card.

transcript.speaker: Dad
transcript.text: Oh yeah, we'll put back your mama card. And they found the jungle, and there they would build the dragon's new home. But we'll have to do that in another Stars and Storytime, because that's the end of that one.

characters.name: Papa Mole
characters.description: The wise leader and industrious architect of the Mischief Moles, this polite elder visionary guides his kin from the attic’s shadowed heights toward the stars and across biting, crystalline ice. A master pathfinder who initiated their voyage upon unearthing an ancient tome, he balances the weight of celestial navigation with the tactile craft of carving a new tunnel home and the thoughtful devotion of planning whimsical days of activities for his children. Whether introducing surprise robots for play, anchoring meditations during a frozen trek, or sleep-sitting in his favorite chair, his tactical brilliance ensures their furthest discoveries remain rooted in the rhythmic, quiet peace of a sanctuary built by his own seasoned hands.

characters.name: Dimitar
characters.description: A sharp-witted resident of a circular island castle, this two-century-old purple dragonet carries a pirate’s salt-crusted legacy and a fierce devotion to his salvaged library. Formerly of the Cadasori galleries, Seeker’s grumpy companion now roams the deep to save his ancestral shores, though a recent volcanic disturbance has left him uncharacteristically sickened and frail. Driven by habit and a tireless appetite for fish, he bickers incessantly over snacks while guarding his remaining volumes with a brooding, protective intensity.

characters.name: Disco
characters.description: An energetic Mischief Mole and superhero-clad detective, Disco is one of two identical twins who embark on grand adventures through the deep. Alongside her sister Thisco, this architect of the subterranean maps hidden realms—from frozen frontiers to the dusty canyons beneath the furniture—by piloting her signature submarine or racing toy cars through the bedroom's wild heights. Guided by "Cornebyborn" dances and Papa Mole’s lore, she finds solace within her glowing basket house, a dream sanctuary and fortress of a hundred locks. Whether navigating grimy domestic landscapes or open waters, Disco balances mechanical ingenuity with a persistent, soft-hearted wonder for the night sky.

characters.name: Sisco
characters.description: The other listener getting ready for bed with Disco.

characters.name: Dragoness
characters.description: The mother dragon whose great nest is mentioned at the start of the book.

characters.name: Isabella
characters.description: A towering Dimatar scholar and alchemist of Atsukeeseet, Mama Izzy is a clever kiddo at heart and a steadfast guardian of rooftop gardens and intricate dollhouses. Gifted with the power of flight, she navigates the open heights with aquatic grace, her massive frame often shielded by a special protective metal that glimmers even as she descends into the shadows. As a resourceful quartermaster, she employs red flashlights, gold-laced slimes, and pipe cleaners to mend a Lanternshark’s failing light, yet her generosity remains her hallmark; she freely shares her vast library, brokers homes for dragons, and teaches others to find the resonance in their own voices. Her most profound magic resides in her vast, soft blanket—a sanctuary of ultimate coziness—where she remains a brave, quick-witted protector to all who honor her singular request for absolute silence.

characters.name: Taylee Mouse
characters.description: A cheese-loving mouse who accompanies Isabella and rides on Dimitar's back.

places.name: Papa Mole's Attic
places.description: Where the ancient book about Dimitar was stored.

places.name: High Mountains
places.description: The cold, lofty location where the dragon nest is situated.

places.name: Dragon Nest
places.description: A structure made of strawberries, feathers, gold, glittery branches, and sea grass.

places.name: The Jungle
places.description: Dimitar’s sanctuary is a sweltering, vapor-thick forest of towering trees and emerald vines, where he builds his home beneath a heavy canopy that shelters kaleidoscopic butterflies and the enigmatic Cindy-Wan the Frog-a-loza. This humid wilderness, teeming with chattering monkeys and a roaming elephant, serves as a wild transit for racers who must navigate its dense, wet depths twice.

places.name: Izzy's Closet
places.description: The location where Isabella keeps her important jungle map picture.
